name: Cantonese Egg Foo Yung
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mushrooms in water for 30 minutes or until soft. Discard the tough stems and drain.
2. Dice the water chestnuts and finely shred the other solid ingredients into pieces the size of a matchstick.
3. Beat the eggs.
4. Heat a wok or skillet over high heat, add 1 tbsp of oil and salt.
5. Just before the oil begins to smoke, add all of the vegetables and roast pork. Stir vigorously for 30 seconds.
6. Add sugar, sherry, and pepper. Cover and cook for 45 more seconds.
7. Remove the mixture and let cool.
8. Add the beaten eggs to the mixture and stir well.
9. Reheat the pan. Add remaining oil.
10. Pour the egg mixture into the pan and fry for one minute on each side or until the omelet has set (or make smaller individual omelets).
11. Serve immediately on a warm platter. This version is served without gravy.
